texto: After asking for votes by presenting Krishna Prasad Bhattarai as the prime ministerial candidate in Nepal’s 2056 BS general election, Girija Prasad Koirala himself became prime minister after the victory.
veredito: Misleading : Although the Nepali Congress presented Krishna Prasad Bhattarai as its prime ministerial candidate during the 2056 BS (1999) general election, he did become prime minister after the party won the election. Girija Prasad Koirala only assumed the premiership later after Bhattarai resigned from the position.

video showing people and Nepal Police carrying election materials through a dangerous cliff path is from Ichchhakamana Rural Municipality, Chitwan
veredito: Misleading. The video is not from Ichchhakamana Rural Municipality in Chitwan. Fact-checking confirms it was recorded in Sisne Rural Municipality–2 of Rukum East on Falgun 18, three days before the election.

texto: A social media post claims that Sunil Sharma stopped providing free treatment services at his hospital after losing the election.
veredito: Misleading.The claim that Sunil Sharma discontinued free medical services at his hospital after losing the election is misleading. Nepal’s health regulations require hospitals to allocate at least 10% of their beds for free treatment to poor, helpless, or abandoned patients under the Health Institution Operation Standard 2077. Therefore, such services cannot legally be discontinued. Additionally, Sharma clarified through his official social media accounts that discounted and free healthcare services for economically disadvantaged patients are still being provided and have not been stopped.

texto: A video circulating on social media claims that K. P. Sharma Oli appeared in the media with tears in his eyes after losing the 2082 House of Representatives election.
veredito: Misleading. The viral video is not related to the 2082 election results. It is actually a clip from an interview given by K. P. Sharma Oli about nine years ago when he was leaving the post of Prime Minister. The clip has been shared with a new and misleading caption claiming that he appeared tearful after losing the election. In the original interview, Oli talks about his health, sleep schedule, reading habits, and dreams; there is no mention of the recent election or any emotional reaction to defeat.
tags: ['KP Sharma Oli', 'Election contest', 'Video', 'House of Representatives', 'communications media', '2082', 'Social Media', 'Eye']

texto: An 18-second viral video clip circulating on social media claims that CPN-UML chairman KP Sharma Oli predicted that only seven seats would be won in the House of Representatives election.
veredito: Misleading. The viral 18-second video clip is taken from a longer interview uploaded on November 17, 2022, on Kantipur’s official YouTube channel. In the original context, KP Sharma Oli was referring to the number of seats the Maoist party might win in the direct election, not predicting the result of the current House of Representatives election. The clip has been shared out of context, creating a misleading impression.

veredito: Misleading. The viral video does not show a gunfire incident in Dhading. It is actually footage from a security drill conducted in Okhaldhunga at the premises of Nepal Red Cross Society, which has been shared with a false claim about election violence in Dhading.

The government gave 7 days of public holidays for the election, and in India, elections are held only on Saturdays or Sundays.
veredito: Misleading‍. Analysis of official sources shows that for the election on Falgun 21, Nepal’s government granted only 3 days of public holiday, not 7. The additional days (Falgun 18–19, 23–24) were for Holi and International Women’s Day, not election purposes. In India, elections are not restricted to Saturdays or Sundays; they occur on multiple days, as confirmed by the official Indian election schedules for 2014, 2019, and 2024. Therefore, Samaa Thapa’s claim is misleading.

Girija Prasad Koirala only assumed the premiership later after Bhattarai resigned from the position.

Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) Morang leader Durga Regmi died of a heart attack at the vote counting center.
veredito: Misleading. Rastriya Swatantra Party Morang leader Durga Regmi did not die of a heart attack at the vote counting center. Reports indicate he suddenly collapsed while eating near the vote counting venue and was taken to Rangeli Hospital, where doctors declared him dead. The claim that he died at the vote counting center due to a heart attack is therefore misleading.

A video circulating on social media claims that the Nepali Army opened fire in Myagdi after people attempted to capture a polling booth during voting.
veredito: Misleading — The viral video does not show a real incident of booth capture or gunfire during voting in Myagdi. It is actually footage from a joint election security drill conducted in Beni, Myagdi on Falgun 10, which has been shared with a misleading caption.

texto: A viral video claims that gunfire occurred in Dhading during voting and the election was cancelled.
veredito: Misleading. The viral video does not show a gunfire incident in Dhading. It is actually footage from a security drill conducted in Okhaldhunga at the premises of Nepal Red Cross Society, which has been shared with a false claim about election violence in Dhading.

৫৩ আসনে ১০ হাজারেরও কম ভোটের ব্যবধানে হেরেছেন জামায়াত জোটের প্রার্থীরা।
